rulings.Case Title: EAST WEST BANK, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION VS. JOHN E. KING et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Attorney'S Fees And Costs
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the DISCOVERY / LAW and MOTION CALENDAR for Wednesday, Mar-25-2026. LINE 1. ASSIGNEE WOLVERINE ENDEAVORS VIII, LLC's, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY Motion For Attorney'S Fees And Costs. Assignee of Record Wolverine Endeavors VIII, LLC ("Wolverine")'s motion for attorney's fees and costs is granted in part. Wolverine is awarded fees of $379,331.30 and costs of $76,056.20. ***THE COMPLETE RULING HAS BEEN SENT TO THE PARTIES VIA EMAIL*** For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: RAYMOND VINCENT DIGIACOMO JR. VS. HEALTHRIGHT 360 ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Imposing Issue, Evidentiary And Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant Denise Williams; Declaration; And, Memorandum.
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the DISCOVERY / LAW and MOTION CALENDAR for Wednesday, Mar-25-2026. LINE 9. PLAINTIFF RAYMOND DIGIACOMO's Motion For Order Imposing Issue, Evidentiary And Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant Denise Williams. Plaintiff Raymond DiGiacomo Jr.'s motion to impose issue, evidentiary, and monetary sanctions against defendant Denise Williams is denied. Williams was ordered on December 9, 2025, to provide objection-free responses to special interrogatory nos. 26, 27, 29, 30, 35, 36, and 39. Williams's responses contain no objections and comply with the Code. Generally, Williams responds that she does not recall the circumstances of her statement in a prior litigation that HealthRight 360 received certain grievances submitted by DiGiacomo, and she provides the identities of people with information about that assertion (herself and her attorney). Her responses are sufficient and comply with this court's prior order. The court exercises its discretion to consider defendant's late-filed opposition brief. The court concludes that the motion lacked substantial justification and orders plaintiff to pay $100 to defendant within 30 days. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. rulings.Case Title: JEREMY RICARD VS. VELO LABS, INC., A DELAWARE CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: PLAINTIFF JEREMY RICARD'S MOTION TO ENFORCE SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT PURSUANT TO CALIFORNIA CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE SECTION 664.6. (pursuant to Court Order filed 1/27/26)
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the DISCOVERY / LAW and MOTION CALENDAR for Wednesday, Mar-25-2026. LINE 2. PLAINTIFF JEREMY RICARD'S MOTION TO ENFORCE SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT PURSUANT TO CALIFORNIA CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE SECTION 664.6. (pursuant to Court Order filed 1/27/26). Plaintiff Jeremy Ricard's motion to enforce settlement agreement against defendant Velo Labs, Inc. is granted. Judgment shall enter against this defendant in accordance with the terms of the settlement agreement attached as Exhibit A to the November 12, 2025 Ruddell Declaration. Ricard shall submit a proposed judgment. The motion is denied without prejudice as to individual defendant Jack Al-Kahwati in light of the bankruptcy stay. Plaintiff may refile when the stay is lifted. Al-Kahwati submitted a separate brief asking to delay entry of judgment against Velo Labs in light of the fact that he is an owner of this corporation. This request is denied; the automatic stay extends only to the debtor who is a party to bankruptcy proceedings and not to separate entities owned by the debtor. (See, e.g., New Orleans Gas & Elec. Lights, Inc. v. Me'lange Joli, Inc. (M.D. La., July 8, 2011, No. CIV.A. 11-194-JJB-CN) 2011 WL 2680578, at *2 ["a corporation which is wholly owned by a bankruptcy debtor is considered a separate and distinct legal entity, and, as such, actions against the corporation are not subject to the stay"]; In re Calhoun (Bankr. N.D. Iowa 2004) 312 B.R. 380, 384 ["The automatic stay does not stay actions against separate entities associated with the debtor."].) For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: ERIC LEE VS. RICARDO PALOMINO MORENO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To Conduct A Neuropsychological Examination Of Plaintiff
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the DISCOVERY / LAW and MOTION CALENDAR for Wednesday, Mar-25-2026. LINE 5. DEFENDANT THE ESTATE OF RICARDO PALOMINO MORENO's, DECEASED, Motion For Leave To Conduct A Neuropsychological Examination Of Plaintiff. Defendant's motion for leave to conduct a neuropsychological examination of plaintiff is granted. Plaintiff's claimed damages and harms from the accident include cognitive and memory issues. (Bednar Decl., Ex. B.) Defendant's notice of examination adequately identifies the tests sought and Plaintiff raises no objection to any test on the enumerated list. Defendant's moving papers show good cause for the test conditions set out in the notice of examination. Plaintiff may contest the tentative and avoid the examination if he is willing to stipulate to the CCP 2032.320(c) stipulation. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. rulings.Case Title: MARCO AHZI MOXQUUZ VS. FCA US LLC, A DELAWARE LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Compel Deposition Of Plaintiff Marco Ahzi Moxquuz And Request For Sanctions For Misuse Of The Discovery Process
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the DISCOVERY / LAW and MOTION CALENDAR for Wednesday, Mar-25-2026. LINE 6. 1-DEFENDANT FCA US LLC's Motion To Compel Deposition Of Plaintiff Marco Ahzi Moxquuz And Request For Sanctions For Misuse Of The Discovery Process. The motion to compel plaintiff's deposition is granted. Plaintiff shall sit for deposition by April 9, 2026. Although plaintiff has now provided dates, his repeated failure to provide dates in response to reasonable requests lacked substantial justification. The dates he has provided are beyond the discovery cutoff based on the July 27, 2026 trial date. The court orders plaintiff and his counsel to pay $2060 in sanctions to defendant within 30 days of this order. The court awards the sanctions jointly and severally against counsel because it appears that it was counsel's failure to meet and confer about deposition dates that constituted misuse of the discovery process. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.)
